runs.text: A small, real step. Set up a standing order, open a new savings account, or move a set amount.
Slow leaks often show up in the heavier column again and again, and you may not notice the pattern. Once you can see the pattern, you can decide what to keep, cut back, or drop.

At the same time, caregiving needs steady attention: watching, responding, adjusting. This adds to the physical load. Touch overload and tired attention combine. That is why a high-contact day with small children tires you out more than the activity alone would explain.
One shared, real money document: the monthly budget, a bank summary, a savings statement, or the credit-card bill. Not made-up numbers.
Name the exact body signals. Not just "anxious", but where you feel it and how it feels.
Write the exact thing. Not tiredness in general, but the one cost that no one noticed.

The clearest finding in forgiveness research is simple. The person who gains from forgiveness is the one who forgives. Long-held resentment keeps the stress response on: raised cortisol, higher blood pressure, a weaker immune system, lower mood. Forgiveness calms that response. Whether the other person ever knows is not the point.

A quiet resentment of the body, because it lets you down in ways you can see. Always on alert: tracking how sleepy you are, counting hours, dreading the gap between getting into bed and falling asleep. Shame, because people who sleep well don't really get it. Many quietly believe they should have been able to fix it by willpower alone.
This is not a flaw in women who end up friendship-lonely. It is the expected result of several life conditions working together.
The usual anxiety toolkit is a good start: breathwork, tapping, less caffeine and alcohol, steady sleep. These calm the nervous system directly, whatever the cause.

The avoidance-rumination cycle is well known in CBT and in anxiety research. The debt-avoidance pattern here is adapted from Day 6 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program. That day treats debt obsession as a pattern that tapping can break.
